description: The Pittsburgh crime family, also known as the LaRocca crime family, was an Italian American Mafia crime family based in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. Formed in the late 1880s under Salvatore Catanzaro, it rose to prominence during Prohibition and was led for decades by Sebastian LaRocca. The family engaged in racketeering, labor union infiltration, and drug trafficking, but became defunct after convictions and attrition, with the last known member dying in 2021.

country_links.evidence_quote: LaRocca also brought the Pittsburgh Mafia into an agreement with the Trafficante crime family of Tampa to manage the Sans Souci casino in Havana .
country_links.context: The Pittsburgh crime family partnered with the Trafficante crime family to manage the Sans Souci casino in Havana.

country_links.evidence_quote: Anthony 'Wango' Capizzi – a former soldier who operated in Las Vegas with the Bufalino family.
country_links.context: The Pittsburgh crime family operated in Las Vegas with the Bufalino family.
